title: The Jungle People (Berkeley Daily Gazette, February 17, 1942)
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: An early Philip K. Dick short piece titled 'The Jungle People,' published in the Berkeley Daily Gazette on February 17, 1942. This is among the earliest known published writings by PKD, who would have been approximately thirteen years old at the time. It represents a significant archival item documenting PKD's juvenilia and earliest public writing efforts prior to his professional science fiction career.
page_summary:
A 2-page scanned PDF of 'The Jungle People,' an early piece by Philip K. Dick published in the Berkeley Daily Gazette on February 17, 1942. No OCR text layer is present. Dick would have been approximately thirteen years old at the time of publication, making this among his earliest known published writings.

The Berkeley Daily Gazette was Dick's local newspaper during his childhood and adolescence in Berkeley, California. Publication at age thirteen demonstrates Dick's precocious literary ambition and the early development of his distinctive imaginative vision. The title 'The Jungle People' suggests an adventure or exotic fiction theme typical of juvenile writing. This document is of considerable biographical interest as evidence of Dick's earliest creative development and his initial entry into the world of published writing.
